label: PI-123 build-closure — unified multi-engagement DB cutover (Stages 1-4); refinements DEC; resolves PI-123

session.session_title: PI-123 build-closure — unified multi-engagement DB cutover (Stages 1-4)
session.session_executive_summary: PI-123 build + cutover execution (Stages 1-4, 06-02-26): landed the unified multi-engagement DB. Made the ORM express the strict schema (composite (engagement_id, identifier) keys, NOT NULL FK), flipped the test suite to active row-level scoping, built + validated the consolidation, and cut the runtime over to data/v2-unified.db with per-request scoping. Built + validated the live unified DB (CRMBUILDER folded in with exact parity; CBM re-created fresh empty). Surfaced and fixed a real correctness bug — per-sessionmaker scope registration left some sessions writing NULL engagement_id — by registering the filter/stamp on the base ORM Session class. Records the build-discovered refinements as DEC-376 and resolves PI-123, which unblocks PI-122.
session.session_description: PI-123 build-closure: ingested the 9 slice + cutover commits (Slice 1 through Stage 4) as CM records, recorded the build-discovered refinements as DEC-376 (string-identifier discriminator; consolidation-coupled enforce; Session-class scope registration fixing the per-sessionmaker un-stamped-write bug; get_engine lock + install-before-publish; CBM re-created fresh; deferred activation-worker de-file), and resolved PI-123 — which unblocks PI-122. Built + validated the live unified DB and cut the runtime over to v2-unified.db with per-request row-level scoping.

conversation.conversation_purpose: Close out PI-123: ingest the slice + cutover commits, record the build-discovered refinements as a decision, and resolve PI-123.
conversation.conversation_description: Executed PI-123 Stages 2-4 (strict-schema flip, consolidation + leak-test, runtime cutover to v2-unified.db), built + validated the live unified DB (CRMBUILDER folded in, CBM fresh), fixed the Session-class scope-registration bug, and authored this build-closure.
conversation.conversation_summary: Resolved PI-123; recorded refinements as DEC-376; unblocked PI-122.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-123 Slice 1 — fold the engagements registry into the unified DB schema (migration 0037)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-123 Slice 1 — fold the engagements registry into the unified DB schema (migration 0037)

WTK-026 / Development phase Slice 1 of the unified multi-engagement-DB
migration (pi-123-unified-db-architecture.md §8 / D1). Additive, non-breaking
first step: defines the engagements tenant table on the main `Base` and adds
migration 0037 to the main chain to create it, so the unified DB holds the
engagement registry the separate meta DB held before.

- access/models.py: add `EngagementRow` to the main `Base` (column-for-column
  identical to access/meta_models.py's `EngagementRow`); import `text` for the
  LOWER() expression indexes.
- migrations/versions/0037: create the engagements table (10 cols, 2 CHECKs,
  2 unique LOWER() indexes, 3 plain indexes); idempotent (skips if present),
  reversible (downgrade drops it). Safe on live per-engagement DBs — adds an
  empty, unused table; the legacy runtime keeps serving /engagements from the
  meta DB until the Deployment cutover.
- tests: `test_engagements_model_parity` pins the two definitions equal so the
  transitional duplication cannot drift; `test_0037_creates_and_drops_*` runs
  0037 in isolation (stamp 0036 -> upgrade -> downgrade), sidestepping the
  decommissioned catalog YAMLs the full-chain tests need.
- Updated `test_meta_db_separate_from_engagement_db`: the per-engagement DB now
  carries the folded-in (empty) engagements table; the invariant becomes data
  isolation between the two physical DBs, not table absence.

Build-discovered refinement: the design's Slice 1 "retire the meta engine,
serve /engagements from the one engine" is staged to the Deployment cutover —
in the per-engagement-file world the registry must stay shared, so Slice 1 is
schema-prep only. Captured here for the build-closure design-doc update on main.

Full v2 suite: 2279 passed; the 18 apply_close_out failures in a branch run are
the apply-on-main branch guard, not regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-123 Slice 2a — add the nullable engagement_id discriminator to scoped tables (migration 0038)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-123 Slice 2a — add the nullable engagement_id discriminator to scoped tables (migration 0038)

WTK-027 (part 1 of Slice 2). Additive, non-breaking schema step toward the
unified multi-engagement DB (pi-123-unified-db-architecture.md §6 / D2): every
engagement-scoped table gains a nullable `engagement_id`. NULL on every existing
row, so nothing changes in the current single-engagement-per-file runtime.

- access/models.py: `EngagementScopedMixin` (engagement_id VARCHAR(32), nullable)
  applied to all 30 scoped models; catalog_* (system/shared) and engagements
  (tenant table) deliberately excluded.
- migrations/0038: add the column to the 30 scoped tables; idempotent + reversible.
- Discriminator decision (refines DEC-375's "integer surrogate" sub-point): the
  column holds the engagement's stable identifier (engagements.engagement_identifier,
  ENG-NNN) — never renamed, so it serves the durable-key role without a separate
  integer surrogate, and stays consistent with v2's identifier-keyed model. The
  FK + NOT NULL + composite (engagement_id, identifier) uniqueness + indexes land
  in Slice 3's batch rebuild, keeping model and migration aligned at each step.
- The central read-filter / write-stamp that key on this column are Slice 2b;
  they stay dormant until the Data Migration backfill.

Tests: test_0038 (model presence; migration-list-vs-models parity guard;
create_all->drop->stamp->upgrade exercise of the real ALTER). Updated 13 entity
shape tests (+engagement_id) and test_pools_isolated (folded-in table now present
but empty — data isolation, not absence).

Full v2 suite: 2282 passed; the 18 apply_close_out failures are the apply-on-main
branch guard, not regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-123 Slice 2b — central engagement-scope mechanism (ContextVar + filter + stamp)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-123 Slice 2b — central engagement-scope mechanism (ContextVar + filter + stamp)

WTK-027 (part 2 of Slice 2). The dormant runtime machinery that makes one
multi-tenant DB behave per-engagement by filtering rows instead of selecting a
file (pi-123-unified-db-architecture.md / D5). New self-contained module
access/engagement_scope.py:

- active-engagement ContextVar (holds the ENG-NNN identifier) + set/get/reset +
  an active_engagement() context manager.
- read filter: a do_orm_execute listener injecting
  with_loader_criteria(EngagementScopedMixin, engagement_id == active) into every
  ORM SELECT — including bare column selects (select(Model.identifier)), so the
  per-engagement identifier-assignment helpers stay correct with zero
  per-repository change.
- write stamp: a before_flush listener stamping engagement_id on new scoped rows
  from the active context (explicit values win, e.g. the data-migration backfill).
- unset guard: optional process-wide enforcement that raises on a scoped
  read/write with no active engagement; off by default.
- install_engagement_scope(session_factory) registers both handlers (idempotent);
  uninstall for tests.

Dormant by default: with no active engagement set, the filter and stamp no-op,
so this changes nothing in the current single-engagement-per-file runtime. It is
imported by nothing in the live app yet — Slice 2c wires the X-Engagement
middleware that sets the ContextVar per request, and the cutover turns on
enforcement.

12 unit tests: stamp, read filter, dormancy, A/B isolation (no cache bleed),
column-select filtering, explicit-value precedence, enforcement on read/write,
non-scoped-entity pass-through, idempotent install, context nesting. De-risked
that with_loader_criteria matches via the non-mapped mixin base.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-123 Slice 2c — per-request engagement-scope wiring (X-Engagement middleware + flag)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-123 Slice 2c — per-request engagement-scope wiring (X-Engagement middleware + flag)

WTK-027 (part 3, completing Slice 2). Wires the dormant scope mechanism (2b)
into the live API behind a default-off flag (pi-123-unified-db-architecture.md
/ D6).

- config: Settings.engagement_scoping_enabled (default False). Default keeps the
  machinery dormant until the unified-DB cutover — turning it on against the
  current NULL-discriminator rows would filter every row out.
- access/db.py: install the scope filter/stamp on the session factory when the
  flag is on (gated, so the default runtime gets no extra session events).
- api/scope_middleware.py: a pure-ASGI middleware (not BaseHTTPMiddleware, whose
  child task drops ContextVar propagation) that resolves the active engagement
  per request — X-Engagement header (identifier ENG-NNN or code), marker-file
  fallback — and sets it on the engagement-scope ContextVar for the request.
  Pass-through when the flag is off.
- api/main.py: register the middleware after the marker guard.

Enforcement (the unset fail-loud guard) is intentionally NOT enabled here — it
is a cutover-hardening step, so 2c needs no new exception handler.

8 tests: resolver (code/identifier/marker/unknown), middleware ContextVar
set+reset (flag on) and pass-through (flag off), and end-to-end — GET /references
with X-Engagement returns only that engagement's rows; no header → dormant (all
rows). api+access+integration suites: 1273 passed, no regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-123 Slice 3 — enforce plan (constraint-landscape analysis) + collision-coexistence proof
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-123 Slice 3 — enforce plan (constraint-landscape analysis) + collision-coexistence proof

WTK-028. Slice 3's deliverable is the design half (the non-obvious work) plus an
empirical proof of the PI's headline crux — not a live migration, because the
analysis shows the enforce is consolidation-coupled, not an independently
mergeable chain head.

- pi-123-slice3-enforce-plan.md: maps the 30 scoped tables into three constraint
  classes (19 identifier-as-PK needing composite-PK rebuilds; 9 surrogate-PK
  UNIQUE-swaps incl. refs' two uniques + charter/status version series;
  2 no-identifier NOT-NULL+FK-only). Finding: a NOT-NULL/composite enforce
  cannot lazy-apply to the live NULL-discriminator DBs (would break activation),
  and the backfill is engagement-aware — so the target schema is reached by the
  consolidation building the unified DB fresh at the strict schema and copying
  rows in with engagement_id (D9), which sidesteps the 19 in-place PK rebuilds.
  The model-strictness flip + fixture-stamping + consolidation + enforcement are
  one coherent cutover, not separable pre-cutover slices.
- test_engagement_id_collision_coexistence.py: builds a target-schema DB
  (representative of each class) and proves CBM SES-001 and CRMBUILDER SES-001
  coexist while intra-engagement duplicates, NULL engagement_id, and unknown-FK
  inserts are all rejected. 8 tests green.

Live ORM models stay nullable (dormant suite unaffected); the strict-schema flip
lands with the cutover unit. The refs same-engagement assertion (D4) is bundled
there too (a no-op while scoping is dormant).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-123 cutover Stage 1 — get_by_identifier helper; convert 64 identifier-as-PK session.get sites
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-123 cutover Stage 1 — get_by_identifier helper; convert 64 identifier-as-PK session.get sites

Behaviour-preserving prep for the composite-(engagement_id, identifier) keying.
The 19 identifier-as-PK governance/methodology tables can't keep the identifier
as the sole primary key once two engagements must both hold e.g. SES-001, so
every session.get(Model, identifier) — a positional primary-key load — must
become a get-by-identifier-column query that the engagement-scope read filter
isolates per engagement.

- access/_helpers.py: get_by_identifier(session, model, id_column, identifier)
  — select(model).where(id_column == identifier).scalar_one_or_none(). Equivalent
  to session.get() today (identifier is still the PK); survives the composite-PK
  change because it keys on the column, not PK position, and the scope filter
  returns the row in the active engagement (or None).
- 64 call sites across 20 repositories converted from
  session.get(<IdentifierPKModel>, identifier) to get_by_identifier(...). The
  surrogate-id PK gets (decisions/topics by numeric id, etc.) are correctly left
  as session.get.

No schema change, no behaviour change: access+api suites 1267 passed, zero
regressions. This removes the blast radius from the upcoming strict-schema flip
(Stage 2). Pre-existing C408 dict()-literal lints in the touched repos are left
as-is (not introduced here).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-123 cutover kickoff — handoff doc for Stages 2-4 + build-closure
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-123 cutover kickoff — handoff doc for Stages 2-4 + build-closure

Self-contained session kickoff so a fresh Claude Code session can continue
PI-123's cutover. Captures: orientation + what's done (Slices 1-3 + cutover
Stage 1, all dormant/pushed); the build-discovered refinements (string-identifier
discriminator, consolidation-coupled enforce); the detailed Stage 2 (strict-schema
flip + central fixture stamp + the constraint-class changes + expected fallout),
Stage 3 (consolidation script + leak-test against copies), and Stage 4 (live
destructive cutover, Doug-in-the-loop); the build-closure on main (complete
WTK-026/027/028 + WSK-009, resolve PI-123, unblock PI-122, author the refinement
DEC); and the working-rule gotchas (branch protocol, meta_export_dir test
pollution, pure-ASGI middleware).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-123 cutover Stage 2 (strict-schema flip) + Stage 3 (consolidation + leak-test)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-123 cutover Stage 2 (strict-schema flip) + Stage 3 (consolidation + leak-test)

Stage 2 — strict (target) schema in the ORM, built by create_all:
- EngagementScopedMixin: engagement_id NOT NULL + FK to
  engagements.engagement_identifier. New EngagementScopedPKMixin makes
  engagement_id a composite-PK member for the 19 identifier-as-PK tables
  (+ engagement_areas). Class B tables swap UNIQUE(identifier) for
  UNIQUE(engagement_id, identifier) (decisions, risks, planning_items, topics,
  charter/status versions, reference_book_versions, refs' two uniques,
  commits' sha); reference_book_versions gains a composite FK; change_log /
  identifier_reservations get NOT NULL + FK + engagement_id-leading index.
- Scope mechanism made robust: install_engagement_scope now registers the
  read-filter / write-stamp on the base ORM Session class (process-wide), not
  per-sessionmaker. Per-factory registration left some sessions un-stamped
  (NULL engagement_id under the strict schema) — the root cause of an
  intermittent, high-rate test flaky. get_engine also builds the factory under
  a lock and installs scope before publishing it.
- Resolver reads the unified engagements table (engagement.list_engagements_unified,
  wired into scope_middleware); the scope middleware is now the outermost
  middleware so the ContextVar is set before the marker-guard BaseHTTPMiddleware.
- engagement_areas repo: session.get(name) -> scoped select (composite PK).
- Test fixtures run under active scoping: conftest.v2_env seeds ENG-001 (FK
  target) + sets active engagement + enforcement; api client carries an
  X-Engagement header. Fixed the resulting fallout across access/api/migration
  (shape PK assertions, concurrent-worker active-engagement, raw-SQL inserts,
  the obsolete dormant-write test, the meta-DB separation tests, the 0038
  model-strictness assertion).

Stage 3 — consolidation + the cross-engagement leak-test:
- migration/unify_engagement_dbs.py: build a fresh unified DB at the strict
  schema, seed engagements from the meta DB, copy each source's scoped rows with
  engagement_id stamped (surrogate-id tables offset per engagement; decisions/
  topics self-FKs offset with them), copy catalog once, validate count +
  identifier parity, no NULL engagement_id, and PRAGMA foreign_key_check.
- migration/test_engagement_leak_isolation.py (§7 acceptance backbone) and
  migration/test_unify_engagement_dbs.py (consolidation harness over synthetic
  colliding-identifier sources).

Stage 4 runbook: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/pi-123-stage4-cutover-runbook.md —
documents the destructive cutover + the pre-flight finding that the live sources
(CRMBUILDER@0036, CBM@0010) lack engagement_id and must be chain-migrated to head
before consolidation (catalog-YAML gap to resolve).

Tests: 1291 passed; the lone failure (test_0037_creates_and_drops_engagements_table)
is pre-existing on the branch (alembic stamp-then-upgrade vs the gitignored
catalog YAMLs), confirmed to fail on HEAD models too.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-123 cutover Stage 4 — runtime routes to the unified DB (row-level scoping)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-123 cutover Stage 4 — runtime routes to the unified DB (row-level scoping)

The runtime now binds to the single unified multi-engagement DB instead of a
per-engagement file; engagement selection is row-level (the scope middleware
resolves the active engagement per request and the central filter/stamp scope
every query).

- runtime/engagement_routing.route_settings_to_engagement: point
  CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH at data/v2-unified.db (not engagement_db_path(code)) and
  enable scoping; the engagement's export dir stays per-engagement (D7). This is
  the single chokepoint both the API (cli.run_api) and the desktop activation
  call, so both inherit the unified binding.
- config.db_path default → data/v2-unified.db (the ancient v2.db default is
  retired; tests override via env).
- conftest.v2_env writes the current_engagement.json marker so the resolver's
  no-header fallback resolves the default engagement — covering API-backed tests
  including the UI panels' StorageClient-over-TestClient fixtures (no header),
  mirroring how the desktop marker drives it in production.
- Test updates for the new model: routing/launcher tests assert the unified DB
  + scoping (not per-engagement files); test_app_engagement_routing rewritten to
  assert row-level isolation (both engagements share one DB, scoped by
  engagement_id); two active-engagement-context tests clear the seeded marker to
  exercise their no-marker paths.

The live unified DB was built + validated offline via
migration/unify_engagement_dbs.py: CRMBUILDER (ENG-001) consolidated with exact
per-table count/identifier parity (8931 scoped rows, FK-check clean, no NULL
engagement_id); CBM (ENG-002) re-created fresh as an empty engagement (its 16
stale chain-0010 rows discarded, per decision). Smoke-tested end to end: reads
scope per engagement (CRMBUILDER 121 sessions / CBM 0), writes stamp + assign
identifiers per engagement, zero errors.

Tests green: api 413, ui 854, runtime+integration 20, access/migration per prior
commit. DEFERRED to follow-up (per the Stage 4 runbook): retire the meta engine /
its chain + serve /engagements from the unified table, remove the now-unused
per-engagement files + meta DB after a rollback window, the full per-request D7
export, and the __version__ bump. The meta DB + per-engagement files remain on
disk as the rollback set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

decisions.title: PI-123 build-discovered refinements — string-identifier discriminator, consolidation-coupled enforce, Session-class scope registration, CBM re-created fresh
decisions.context: PI-123 (PRJ-019) implemented the unified multi-engagement DB whose design DEC-375 settled (D1-D11). Executing the build (Slices 1-3) and the cutover (Stages 1-4) surfaced points where what shipped differs from, or sharpens, DEC-375; this DEC records them so the architecture doc and the successor program reflect reality.
decisions.decision: (1) The engagement discriminator is the stable string identifier engagements.engagement_identifier (ENG-NNN), NOT a new integer surrogate as DEC-375/D1 said — it is never renamed, so it IS the durable key, with less churn and consistency with v2's identifier-keyed model (refs, etc.). (2) The enforce step (composite (engagement_id, identifier) keys + engagement_id NOT NULL + FK) is NOT an independently-mergeable Alembic chain head; it is reached by building the unified DB fresh at the strict schema (Base.metadata.create_all) and copying rows in with engagement_id stamped (D9), because a NOT-NULL/composite chain head fails on live per-engagement DBs whose rows are NULL until the engagement-aware backfill. (3) The central read-filter (do_orm_execute) and write-stamp (before_flush) are registered on the base ORM Session class (process-wide), NOT per-sessionmaker as Slice 2b did — per-factory registration left sessions created from a not-yet-registered factory writing un-stamped (NULL engagement_id) rows under the strict schema, an intermittent high-rate correctness bug; get_engine also builds + installs the factory under a lock before publishing it. (4) CBM (ENG-002) was re-created fresh as an empty engagement at cutover — its 16 stale rows at Alembic chain 0010 predated the schema's NOT-NULL backfills (executive_summary etc.) and could not migrate cleanly. (5) The desktop engagement-switch activation worker still pre-flight-migrates and binds the per-engagement file (Steps 3/8); switching to CBM currently fails at Step 3. This de-file work is deferred to the successor Production Multi-Tenant API program.
decisions.rationale: Each refinement was forced by build/cutover reality, not preference: the identifier is already the durable key; a strict chain head cannot run on un-backfilled live rows; per-factory event registration is unsafe under the strict NOT NULL once any session misses it; CBM's pre-backfill data is unmigratable without fabricating content; and the activation-worker rewrite is squarely the successor program's de-file scope. Recording them keeps DEC-375 / the architecture doc honest and gives the successor program a precise starting point.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Integer-surrogate discriminator (rejected: churn + inconsistent with v2's identifier model). Enforce as an Alembic chain head 0039 (rejected: breaks live lazy `upgrade head` before the engagement-aware backfill). Per-sessionmaker scope registration (rejected: the un-stamped-write hazard). Migrating CBM's stale chain-0010 rows (rejected: requires fabricating NOT-NULL backfill content for its governance rows; re-creating it empty was chosen instead). Fixing the activation worker inside PI-123 (deferred: it is the successor program's de-file work).
decisions.consequences: pi-123-unified-db-architecture.md D1/D3/D5/D8/D9 are annotated to match. The strict schema + Session-class scoping + the consolidation script + the leak-test ship on the pi-123 branch. CBM starts empty in the unified DB. Engagement switching in the desktop app is broken until the successor program de-files the activation worker (tracked in production-multitenant-api-architecture.md D6 + the Stage-4 runbook). PI-123 is Resolved, unblocking PI-122.
decisions.executive_summary: PI-123's build/cutover surfaced refinements to the DEC-375 design, recorded here. The engagement discriminator is the stable string identifier engagements.engagement_identifier (ENG-NNN), not an integer surrogate. The enforce (composite keys + NOT NULL + FK) is reached by building the unified DB fresh at the strict schema and copying rows in (D9), not as a chain head (which would break live lazy upgrade pre-backfill). The scope read-filter/write-stamp are registered on the base ORM Session class, not per-sessionmaker — the per-factory approach left sessions writing NULL engagement_id under the strict schema. CBM was re-created fresh empty (its stale chain-0010 rows could not migrate). The desktop engagement-switch activation worker remains file-bound (deferred to the successor program).
